close_reason: AI analysis implemented in SDK/CLI providers with structured diff output, wired to /analyze-correction route
description: After human edit, AI analyzes diff and guesses why human version is better. Returns structured analysis: differences array (type, original, corrected, reason), was_ai_wrong flag, summary. User confirms with thumbs up/down.

description: Build the translation phase. For simple text: document-level AI translation in one call (send all paragraphs with [P#] markers + glossary + learnings, get JSON array back). Streaming progress via SDK streaming API + WebSocket. Paragraph alignment: map AI response to source paragraphs. For complex layout: AI translate script endpoint (takes Turkish script + glossary, produces v3_translated.py). Script execution + PDF rendering of translated output. See docs/workbench-v2-design.md Section 5.
title: Workbench v2: Epic 3 — Translation Phase (Document-Level AI Translation)

description: POST /api/translate/:documentId. Load all paragraphs for the document. Build prompt with [P0], [P1] markers + glossary + learnings. One AI call via SDK streaming. Parse JSON response [{p, text}]. Create document_translations row. Create paragraph_alignments rows mapping each paragraph to its translation. Broadcast WebSocket progress as paragraphs complete. Update document translation_status. 30-60s target for full affidavit.
title: Document-level translation endpoint (simple text track)

description: POST /api/scripts/version/:id/translate. Read approved Turkish layout script from disk. Build prompt: 'Translate all Turkish text to English, preserve layout code.' Include glossary + learnings. AI returns complete modified Python script. Save as new script_version with purpose='translated'. Return the new version ID.
title: AI translate script endpoint (complex layout track)

description: New service: document-translator.ts. buildDocumentTranslationPrompt(): construct prompt with rules, glossary section, learned corrections section, numbered paragraphs. buildScriptTranslationPrompt(): construct prompt for translating Python scripts. getLearnings() already exists in learning-extractor.ts — reuse it. Handle token estimation for large documents.
title: Translation prompt builder with glossary + learnings injection

description: Update sdk-provider.ts to use client.messages.stream() instead of client.messages.create(). As AI streams JSON response, detect completed paragraph objects. Broadcast progress per paragraph via WebSocket (translation-progress, translation-stdout, translation-complete events). Parse final JSON from accumulated text. Handle malformed JSON gracefully.
title: Streaming translation progress via SDK streaming API

Fix the broken layout review workflow and UX issues discovered during walkthrough. The layout page is currently a dead end - there's no way to generate translated output from within the workbench, and the UI has multiple usability gaps.
notes: Implemented all 7 child tasks: fixed output path detection in generation.ts (P1), added Generate Document button to Layout empty state (P1), disabled Approve Layout when no output (P2), humanized filenames across all pages (P2), added tooltips to Layout controls (P3), fixed misleading zero-count color coding (P3), added full breadcrumb navigation with cross-links (P3). TypeScript compiles clean.
title: Layout Review Workflow & UX Polish

description:
When the GENERATED OUTPUT panel is empty ('No scripts or runs available'), show a prominent call-to-action button that triggers document generation. This is the critical missing workflow step. The button should:
1. Call the existing generation endpoint to produce a translated .docx from approved translations
2. Show progress via WebSocket (infrastructure exists)
3. On completion, auto-refresh the right panel to show the generated PDF
4. If no translations exist yet, show a message directing user to Review page first

Files: LayoutReviewPage.tsx, generation.ts, layout.ts
Related: generation.ts output path detection is fragile (lines 165-172, uses ../output dir)
title: Add 'Generate Document' button to Layout page empty state

description:
The generation route (generation.ts lines 165-172) tries to find output files in '../output' directory but only stores the directory path, not the actual file. This causes the /run/:runId/download endpoint and PDF conversion to fail.

Fix: After script execution completes, scan the output directory for new files (or parse stdout for the output path), and store the actual file path in the generation_runs table. Consider adding an output_file column to generation_runs.
title: Fix output path detection in generation.ts

description:
The 'Approve Layout' button is prominent and clickable even when the right panel (GENERATED OUTPUT) is empty. This is misleading - there's nothing to approve yet.

Fix: Disable/gray out the button when there are no generation runs for the document. Add a tooltip explaining why it's disabled: 'Generate a document first to enable layout approval'. Re-enable once a generation run exists.

File: LayoutReviewPage.tsx
title: Disable Approve Layout button when no output exists

description:
Raw filesystem names with underscores (TEST_SAMPLE-witness_statement-20240315_original.docx) appear throughout the UI - in the document list page header, layout page header, etc. 

Fix: Parse the filename into a human-friendly display name. E.g. 'Witness Statement' or at minimum replace underscores with spaces and strip the project prefix/date suffix. Apply to:
- Document list page (DocumentListPage.tsx)
- Layout page header (LayoutReviewPage.tsx)
- Any other places the raw filename appears

Consider adding a display_name column to the documents table, or a client-side formatting utility.
title: Humanize document filenames in UI

description:
Several controls on the Layout page have cryptic labels that confuse new users:

1. 'Scripts (0)' - What are scripts? Add tooltip: 'Python generation scripts that produce translated documents'
2. 'Sync ON' (green button) - Sync what? Rename to 'Page Sync' or 'Sync Pages' with tooltip: 'Synchronize page navigation between original and output panels'
3. 'Simple Text' badge on doc list - unexplained. Add tooltip: 'Document workflow type'
4. Feedback panel says 'Enable Annotate and draw on the right PDF' when no right PDF exists - show contextual message instead

File: LayoutReviewPage.tsx, DocumentListPage.tsx
title: Add tooltips and improve labeling for Layout page controls

description:
On the document list page, the stats line shows '0 approved' in green and '0 rejected' in red. Green '0 approved' reads as positive when it's actually bad (nothing is approved yet). Red '0 rejected' reads as alarming when it's actually fine (nothing was rejected).

Fix: Only color-code non-zero values. Zero counts should be gray/neutral. Or show a progress bar instead: '0/8 paragraphs reviewed'.

File: DocumentListPage.tsx
title: Fix misleading color coding on document stats

- Layout page: Projects > TEST SAMPLE > Witness Statement > Layout Review
- Review page: Projects > TEST SAMPLE > Witness Statement > Translation Review
- Add cross-links: from Layout page, link to Review page and vice versa, without going back to document list

Files: LayoutReviewPage.tsx, TranslationReviewPage.tsx
title: Add full breadcrumb navigation

description:
## Context
Documents with embedded images containing Turkish text labels need translation. The numbered legend approach (Phase 4b) won't work because:
- **Requirement: NO Turkish or foreign language can appear on final documents**
- Legends still show original Turkish text alongside translations

## Required Approach: Inpainting + Text Replacement
Must use the "inpainting" approach to:
1. Detect text regions in images (OCR)
2. Remove/inpaint over original Turkish text
3. Render English translation in same position
4. Result: Clean image with only English text

## Recommended Models (from research)
| Step | Model | Link |
|------|-------|------|
| Layout Detection | Surya or cmarkea/detr-layout | See docs/notes/research.md |
| Text Detection | manga-ocr or EasyOCR | - |
| Inpainting | LaMa (big-lama) | https://huggingface.co/smartywu/big-lama |
| Full Pipeline | manga-image-translator | https://github.com/zyddnys/manga-image-translator |

## Test Case
- Fire investigation report: projects/BILEN_ABDURRAHIM-20170329/
- Has photos with callout labels that need Turkish removed and English added

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Prototype that removes Turkish text from image
- [ ] Inpaints background where text was removed
- [ ] Renders English translation in similar position/style
- [ ] Output looks professional (no obvious artifacts)
- [ ] Document the approach in nextgen guide

## References
- docs/notes/research.md
- docs/notes/image-text-translation-research.md
- docs/document-replication-guide-nextgen.md (Approach 3)
title: Iterate on image text translation - inpainting approach

dispatched_by: mayor

## Context
Final documents need to be delivered in PDF format. The current workflow generates .docx files.

## Requirements
- Output must be PDF (not .docx)
- Uktu's documents cannot have ANY Turkish or foreign language visible
- PyMuPDF supports Optional Content Layers that could allow toggleable text

## Proposed Approach
1. Research PyMuPDF (fitz) for PDF generation capabilities
2. Evaluate if Optional Content Layers work for this use case
3. Duplicate existing workflow structure but output PDF instead of DOCX
4. Key library: PyMuPDF (fitz) - see https://pymupdf.readthedocs.io/

## Reference
- PyMuPDF translation guide: https://medium.com/@pymupdf/translating-pdfs-a-practical-pymupdf-guide-c1c54b024042
- Current DOCX generators in projects/*/scripts/

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PDF generation script that mirrors current DOCX workflow
- [ ] Text content matches existing translations
- [ ] Layout reasonably matches original documents
- [ ] No Turkish text visible in final output (or toggleable via layers)
title: Implement PDF generation workflow with Optional Content Layers

- Header with company info
- Table with employee data, dates, amounts

- OCR to extract text placement
- Recreating table structure in Word
- Python libraries (python-docx, etc.)

description: Paragraph-level correction workflow: when user edits a translated paragraph, store correction (original AI text vs human edit). AI analyzes the diff (guess why). Human confirms/corrects the analysis. Extract patterns into translation_learnings. Inject top learnings into future translation prompts (both document-level and script-level). Re-translation: re-translate single paragraph with full document context + correction. Terminology ripple: detect when a fix should apply to other paragraphs, offer 'Apply to N others'. See docs/workbench-v2-design.md Section 3 Learning Loop.
title: Workbench v2: Epic 5 — Learning Loop & Corrections

description: When user edits a paragraph via POST /api/review/edit/:alignmentId: create paragraph_corrections row (original_ai_text, human_edit). Return correction ID for confirmation step.
title: Paragraph correction workflow: store + analyze diffs

description: Reuse/adapt learning-extractor.ts for paragraph-level. When user confirms AI analysis: extract pattern (wrong → correct, with explanation). Store in translation_learnings table. title: Learning extraction: patterns from confirmed corrections

description: POST /api/translate/paragraph/:alignmentId/retranslate. Build prompt: full document for context + target paragraph source + previous rejected translation + correction details. POST /api/translate/:documentId/apply-fix to batch-replace.

description:
When the project scanner runs, auto-import Python scripts from projects/*/scripts/ into the script_versions table. Match scripts to documents using slug patterns from filenames. Convention: generate_<slug>_docx_v<N>.py → extract version_label=v<N>, purpose=layout. If filename contains '_translated' → purpose=translated. If contains '_with_images' or '_annotated' → purpose=annotated.

Files to modify:
- workbench/src/server/services/project-scanner.ts — add script discovery after document scanning
- Already have: script_versions table, registerScript endpoint, all the DB schema

Key details:
- Scripts dir: projects/<project_slug>/scripts/
- One project may have scripts for multiple documents (match by slug in filename)
- Avoid duplicate imports on re-scan (check if script_path already exists)
- Mark the highest-version layout script as is_current=1
- Example: DEMIR_GOKHAN has v1,v2,v3,v3_translated,v4,v5 scripts for payroll
title: Auto-discover scripts from filesystem on scan

description:
The core missing piece. Add an 'AI Fix Layout' button to the LayoutReviewPage that:

1. Collects all OPEN feedback annotations for the current generation run
2. Reads the current script source (GET /api/generation/script/:scriptId/source endpoint already exists)
3. Sends to Claude CLI: 'Here is a Python script that generates a .docx. Here are layout issues found by the reviewer: [list of feedback with page numbers and descriptions]. Fix the script to address these issues. Return the complete modified Python script.'
4. Saves the AI response as a new script version (version_label auto-incremented, e.g. v3 → v4)
5. Auto-runs the new script (POST /api/generation/run already exists)
6. Marks addressed feedback as 'resolved'

Backend:
- New endpoint: POST /api/ai/fix-layout/:documentId
- Request body: { script_version_id, feedback_ids[] }
- Uses CLI provider (same as translate — spawn claude with CLAUDECODE env stripped)
- Saves new script to projects/<slug>/scripts/generate_<slug>_docx_v<next>.py
- Creates script_version record + triggers generation run

Frontend (LayoutReviewPage.tsx):
- Add 'AI Fix Layout' button in the header bar, next to Approve button
- Only enabled when: there are open feedback items AND a current script exists
- Shows spinner during AI processing (can take 30-60s)
- On completion: reload scripts list, select new run, show result
- Toast/notification on success or error

The prompt should include:
- The full current Python script source
- All open feedback items with page numbers and region descriptions
- The original document filename for context

title: AI Fix Layout button and endpoint

description:
The generation.ts endpoint already broadcasts 'generation-output' and 'generation-complete' WebSocket events during script execution, but the LayoutReviewPage doesn't listen for them. Currently the UI polls every 2s for 60 iterations.

Changes needed in LayoutReviewPage.tsx:
1. Import useWebSocket hook (already exists at src/client/hooks/useWebSocket.ts)
2. Listen for 'generation-output' events → show real-time stdout in a terminal-style output panel (similar to BatchTranslateButton's output modal)
3. Listen for 'generation-complete' events → stop spinner, reload run data, show result
4. Remove or reduce the polling interval (keep as fallback only)
5. The output panel should be a collapsible dark terminal below the PDF viewers, showing script stdout/stderr in real-time

The WebSocket events from generation.ts broadcast:
- { event: 'generation-output', data: { documentId, runId, chunk } }
- { event: 'generation-complete', data: { documentId, runId, status, output_path } }

This gives immediate feedback when running scripts instead of the current silent polling.
title: Wire up WebSocket progress for script execution

description:
When a complex layout document's layout is approved, the generated .docx (from the latest successful generation run) should be parsed into paragraphs. This enables the translation phase for complex docs.

Flow:
1. User clicks 'Approve Layout' on LayoutReviewPage
2. Frontend calls PATCH /api/documents/:id with { layout_status: 'approved' }
3. Backend (documents.ts updateDocument handler) should detect this transition:
   - If workflow_type === 'complex' AND layout_status is changing to 'approved':
   - Find the latest successful generation run (status='completed', has output_path)
   - Call parseDocx(document.id, run.output_path) to extract paragraphs from the GENERATED .docx
   - This is different from simple text docs where we parse the ORIGINAL .docx

Files to modify:
- workbench/src/server/routes/documents.ts — add post-approval parsing logic in the PATCH handler
- The parseDocx function already exists in services/docx-parser.ts

Edge cases:
- If no successful generation run exists, reject the approval with an error message
- If the output .docx doesn't exist on disk, return error
- After parsing, the document should have paragraphs and be ready for 'Translate All'
title: Parse generated .docx on layout approval for complex docs

Add a collapsible code viewer to the Script Panel in LayoutReviewPage.tsx. Backend endpoint already exists: GET /api/generation/script/:scriptId/source returns { source, path, size }.

UI design:

- Clicking it expands a code panel below the script entry (or a modal)

- Read-only view — editing scripts is done by AI Fix Layout or manually on disk

This is useful for:

- Debugging layout issues by reading the script

Keep it simple — a <pre> tag with overflow-auto is fine. No need for Monaco/CodeMirror.
